steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize how harsh tundra conditions can be. The decisive clue in the notes is the specific, extreme temperature detail: winters average a frigid −30°F. Only a choice that names that temperature and pairs it with language signaling severity will accomplish the goal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Winters in the tundra are especially harsh, with temperatures averaging a frigid −30 degrees Fahrenheit.: Uses the explicit −30°F figure alongside the words especially harsh and frigid, directly emphasizing severity. ✓
  • Choice B — Animals that have adapted to harsh winter conditions can survive tundra winters.: Focuses on animal survival, not on the harshness of the conditions themselves. The emphasis is on adaptation, not severity. ✗
  • Choice C — There are around 1,700 different kinds of plants that can live in the tundra, where average temperatures can reach a mild 54 degrees Fahrenheit.: Highlights plant diversity and introduces the word mild, which directly contradicts the goal of emphasizing harshness. ✗
  • Choice D — Along with animals that have adapted to the tundra's conditions, around 1,700 different kinds of plants can live in the tundra.: Emphasizes that life can survive in the tundra, not that conditions are harsh. No temperature or harshness language appears. ✗
That matches Choice A — Winters in the tundra are especially harsh, with temperatures averaging a frigid −30 degrees Fahrenheit..
